The meaning of that promise is evident: “Now, when the multitude had all eaten and drunk, behold, they were filled with…

This verse contains the language of Micah 4:13 "Arise and thresh, O daughter of Zion: for I will make thine horn iron, and I will make thy hoofs brass: and thou shalt beat in pieces many people: and I will consecrate their gain unto the LORD, and their substance unto the Lord of the whole earth."

Jehovah spake through Moses: "I will raise them up a Prophet from among their brethren, like unto thee [Moses], and will put my words in his mouth; and he shall speak unto them all that I shall command him. And it shall come to pass, that whosoever will not hearken unto my words which he shall speak in my name, I will require it of him." (Deuteronomy 18:18-19.) The Apostle Peter, presumably…

And it shall come to pass, that whosoever will not hearken unto my words which he shall speak in my name, I will require it of him." (Deuteronomy 18:18-19.) The Apostle Peter, presumably…
16 And verily, verily, I say unto you, that this is my voice unto all. Amen.
22 And.as they were eating, Jesus took bread and. brake it, and blessed it, and gave to his disciples, and said, Take, eat; this is in remembrance of my body which I give a ransom for you.

22 (return) [ Although there had been a few occasional prophets before,
yet was this Samuel the first of a constant succession of prophets in
the Jewish nation, as is implied in St. Peter's words, Acts 3:24 "Yea,
and all the prophets, from Samuel, and those that follow after, as many
as have spoken, have likewise foretold of those days." See also Acts
13:20. The others were rather sometime called…

3 Nephi 20 (Isaiah 52) Chiasm a. I will remember the covenant which I have made with my people...that I would give unto them again the land of their fathers for their inheritance, which is the land of Jerusalem (20:29)

The “work of the Father” referred to here has to do with fulfilling specific covenants made with the house of Israel. They include the return of the Jews to the land of their inheritance, the gathering of Israel (Isa 43:5-7), the building of a temple in Jerusalem (Isa 44:28), the promise that the Gentiles shall bring thy sons in their arms, and thy daughters shall be carried upon their shoulders…

exalted/extolled/very high. These terms may represent the eternal destiny of the servant, or they may indicate the esteem and regard some will have for him. (Donald W. Parry, Jay A. Parry, and Tina M. Peterson, Understanding Isaiah, p.468)
